title: Asynchronous Processing and Queues Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of asynchronous processing and message queues in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is 'Asynchronous Processing' in a web application?
options: To store all of the application's users.
The technique of offloading long-running, non-critical tasks to a background process so the user receives a response immediately.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: Which of these is a typical use case for asynchronous processing?
options: Changing a user's password.
Sending a welcome email after registration.
Fetching a user's single profile record from the database.
Displaying the current time on the screen.

questions.question: Which of these is the most popular library for implementing background job queues in Node.js using Redis?
options: Express.js
BullMQ
Mongoose
React Router

questions.question: What is 'Idempotency' in the context of background jobs?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
Ensuring that even if a job is accidentally run twice, it doesn't cause any negative or duplicate side effects.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Why is it generally better to use a dedicated message queue rather than just running an asynchronous function in Node.js?
options: It makes the application faster.
It provides persistence; if your server restarts, you won't lose the pending jobs.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To improve the user experience.
